$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `terminal_sessions` repo (Phase-2). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-terminal-sessions-fixture.ts + terminal-sessions-tier2.ts)
and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness terminal_sessions tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: a clean strings-plus-nullables shape — NO boolean and NO
JSON column. Three nullable string columns (`label`, `exitedAt`,
`transcriptPath`) and one nullable REAL-affinity unbounded-int column
(`exitCode`: z.number().int().nullable().optional() — int with no .max() maps
to REAL, so it binds Option<f64>; an integer-valued cell like 137 dumps back
as the JSON integer 137 via js_number_to_json).
v4 CREATE DEFAULTS: none. terminal-sessions' create is a thin _create wrapper
with NO default injection (no default shell/cwd, no startedAt = now). The
create-input schema marks shell/cwd/startedAt optional but the stored
TerminalSessionSchema requires them, so every corpus create pins shell, cwd,
and startedAt explicitly — no nondeterministic default is ever hit.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / image_profiles fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday
pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New fully-populated session: label + transcriptPath set, already exited with non-zero exitCode 137 (exercises integer-valued REAL pass-through). shell/cwd/startedAt pinned explicitly.
New minimal running session: all nullables NULL (label/exitedAt/exitCode/transcriptPath), still active. shell/cwd/startedAt pinned explicitly.
Session exit: seed row 2 (was running) gets exitedAt + exitCode 0 set. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Metadata edit on seed row 1: update label + transcriptPath. updatedAt pinned.
Delete the second created session (the minimal running one).
